title: Chapter Thirty-Two: The World Tree's Roots
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

No one could believe what they had seen.

Masters on both sides stood frozen, staring at the boy holding the severed head of a Tyrant Sword Sect elder. A sight that transcended common sense and stole the fighting spirit from every warrior on the field.

Hyeon Won-chang rose from the dirt. His hero's headband still fluttered at his brow, his tall frame squaring itself with the confidence of a man who had never been thrown. He channelled true energy into his voice -- more power than Yeon-shin had known he possessed -- and the shout rolled across the Guanzhong Plain like a war horn.

"Guest of the Zhongnan Sect! Desolate Sect's Lightning Flash Jeong Yeon-shin has beheaded the Tyrant Sword Sect elder!"

The roar that answered shook the earth.

Young Zhongnan Sect masters erupted. Reverence. Joy. The sound of men and women who had just seen the impossible become real. Yeo Il-sin and the Zhongnan Sword Immortal surged forward with renewed ferocity, driving the bewildered enemy back.

The Tyrant Sword Sect crumbled.

Through the chaos, Yeon-shin held the gaze of the Tyrant Sword Sect Leader. The man stared at him -- not with rage, but with the cold calculation of a predator reassessing its prey.

*Wait.*

A presence hard to look up at. Just meeting his eyes felt like something pressing against his soul.

*Four years should be enough for you too.*

The Tyrant Sword Sect Leader would not die here. Not today.

Yeon-shin turned away.

---

The war was over.

Zhongnan's victory reshaped the balance of power in Shaanxi. The Sword Immortal had cut off the Elder Council Leader's arm and slain five elders. Wei Ji Myo-hwa's name rose again for commanding disciples against veterans twice her age. And stories about a young Desolate Sect swordsman spread from Xi'an like ripples on still water.

A boy of fifteen or sixteen had beheaded a Tyrant Sword Sect elder. Most people scoffed.

"It's enough if the Nine Sects' masters know," Hyeon Won-chang said, slamming his fist on the inn table. He had been drinking. "Let the ignorant say what they will!"

Yeon-shin was not concerned with rumour. He had little interest in matters that did not advance his survival.

*The fruit of the World Tree. That is all that matters.*

"Does fame help with missions?"

"Of course! You can even get an extra drink at inns!"

Wei Ji Myo-hwa smiled at them both over her cup of golden Jingwei Fu tea. "The Beggar Sect will know the truth. They had beggars with lightness techniques watching the battlefield. When you roam the martial world again, the treatment will be different."

The afternoon passed in warm conversation -- talk of the Beggar Sect, of seniority within Desolate Sect (Hyeon Won-chang's eyes bulged at Yeon-shin claiming to be the elder brother), of plans for the journey ahead. The sun set. The atmosphere softened.

The next morning, Ou Yang Chen bestowed the Flowing Cloud Tablet.

The wooden tablet bore the mark of clouds, carved deep. Showing it to any secular sect under Zhongnan would earn supreme courtesy. Since the Jeong family had been a branch of Zhongnan, the doors it opened would be considerable.

"If you need our sword," the Western Heavens Sword Lord said, "do not hesitate. Even for Desolate Sect's political matters, we will help once."

Yeon-shin accepted. He did not refuse gifts that might prove useful for the climb ahead.

Then he turned to Yeo Il-sin.

"It seems it is time to hear it now."

"You have the qualification," the old woman said, and nodded.

---

"Everything originated from the World Tree."

Yeon-shin's hands went still in his lap.

"The divine tree that supports the world," Yeo Il-sin said. "All principles of the myriad things are established through it. It draws deeply from the earth's energy. The Jeong family's land was very fertile. You were major landowners in Xinye County. The divine tree's roots extended far enough to fold distance."

Silence.

"All universally acknowledged miraculous medicines are created in mysterious ways, following the principles of living things. It is a different story from martial arts or techniques."

"Then the reason for the Jeong family's annihilation..."

"The Desolate Sect Lord's power is said to be sustained by eating the World Tree's fruit. The Tyrant Sword Sect and Blood Flame Sect are sects entirely hostile to Desolate Sect. Destroying one small martial family whose land sheltered the World Tree's roots -- that was more than enough reason for them." Her voice dropped. "They say Xinye County has become a wasteland in their hands."

He lost his words.

*The World Tree.*

The cure for the thing killing him. The fruit he had been running toward since his first breath in this body. The very thing that had made his family a target.

The door rattled.

Not the one he kept closed against grief. A different door -- one he had not known existed. Behind it, a monstrous irony coiled like a serpent eating its own tail. His family had died because the World Tree's roots lay beneath their land. And the fruit of that same tree was the only thing that could save his life.

*The living must live.*

He clamped the thought down. Filed away the paradox. Pushed aside the image of the graves on the back mountain.

Yeo Il-sin watched him with a face that wanted to embrace him. "We received a letter from the Desolate Sect Lord. A request to protect the Jeong family. We failed. I am truly sorry -- to you, to Jung-san, to Hye."

"I understand," Yeon-shin said.

His voice was level. The door held.

*Eat the fruit first. Set aside the complicated circumstances. It is of no help to survival.*

He would ask the Desolate Sect Lord the rest when he became the Divine Sword Squad Leader. They said she lived eternally.

---

The sky was cloudless when they stood at Zhongnan Mountain's entrance.

Many had gathered. Jeong Hye clung to his leg and wept. He crouched and spoke to her gently.

"You can come when you have grown. When you become a master, you will be able to visit Desolate Sect."

His second brother Jeong Jung-san stood apart, watching with an expression that held both regret and pride. Yeon-shin gave him a slight nod.

They were not close enough yet for more.

Wei Ji Myo-hwa stepped forward. "Next time, would it be alright if I came?"

"When there is no mission, I should be the one requesting the Sword Dragon's martial arts."

Her lips curved. The Zhongnan Sword Immortal laughed.

"The backwaters of the Yangtze have formed a friendship. Truly delightful for an old man who should start thinking about washing away the dust."

"You seem like you will live another hundred years," Yeon-shin said.

Laughter everywhere. The old man smiled with the warmth of a grandfather.

"Yeon-shin. I hope you achieve your goals."

Words with many meanings. Yeon-shin clasped his fists, then mounted the fine horse Zhongnan had given him. Hyeon Won-chang aligned his horse alongside.

When leaving, he did not look back.

But the weight of the World Tree's roots sat against the base of his skull like a pressure that would not ease.


---
